_purpose: Golden test specs for run_evals.py. This is a grading harness, not an LLM-invoking one — it scores an already-generated findings.json/checks.json (produced by an actual skill run, cold or otherwise) against must_find/must_not_find/expected_not_applicable expectations. JSON, not YAML, to keep this repo's zero-third-party-dependency policy (PyYAML isn't available/installed by default and every other script here is pure stdlib).

cases.description: Every deliberately planted issue in examples/sample-service (docs-bad scenario) must survive as a finding. Source of truth for this list: examples/sample-service/README.md's planted-issues table.

must_find.note: JPA @Id vs composite DDL key mismatch
partition list has a hardcoded horizon with no maintenance job

allowed_optional.note: the unplanted partition-key-vs-archival-filter mismatch one prior run found independently — nice if present, not required

cases.description: system-context classification (cli-tool) must suppress false-positive risk findings for a system type that legitimately has no throughput/observability surface. This is the golden case for the check-coverage model's not-applicable path — see examples/minimal-cli-tool.expected.md.

must_not_find.note: missing scale target must NOT be a risk for a cli-tool
missing observability must NOT be a risk for a cli-tool
